stories.title: Making Tax Digital for Income Tax: First Quarterly Update Due 7 August 2026
stories.summary: HMRC has reminded agents that clients' first quarterly update under Making Tax Digital for Income Tax must be submitted by 7 August 2026. Agents can use their compatible software to send updates directly to HMRC.

stories.title: HMRC Making Slow Progress on Agents' 'Pain Points'
stories.summary: ICAEW reports that HMRC's updated transformation roadmap suggests digital services for tax agents may continue to lag behind those for taxpayers for some time, raising concerns about efficiency for professionals.

stories.title: Sir Andy Marsh Named as Preferred Candidate for HM Chief Inspector of Constabulary
stories.summary: The Home Secretary confirmed Sir Andy Marsh QPM as the government's preferred candidate for the role of HM Chief Inspector of Constabulary and Fire & Rescue Services, announced on 7 July 2026.

stories.title: FCDO Summons Iranian Chargé d'Affaires Over Attack on Iranian Journalist
stories.summary: The FCDO summoned Iran's Chargé d'Affaires in London following the sentencing of two individuals in connection with an attack on an Iranian international journalist, signalling the UK's strong condemnation of the incident.

stories.title: New HM Ambassador Appointed to the United Arab Emirates
stories.summary: Mrs Geraldine McCafferty has been appointed as His Majesty's Ambassador to the United Arab Emirates, succeeding Mr Edward Hobart in the role.

stories.title: Family Calls for Public Inquiry into DWP Failings After Disabled Woman's Death
stories.summary: The mother of a disabled woman who died by suicide after her benefits were incorrectly stopped has called for a full public inquiry into DWP failings. The case has reignited debate about the safety of the UK benefits system.

stories.title: Carer's Allowance: Government Call for Evidence
stories.summary: The DWP has issued a call for evidence on Carer's Allowance, gathering views on how to improve the system. Recent steps include linking the weekly earnings limit to 16 times the National Living Wage hourly rate.

agencies.name: DEFRA
agencies.description: Department for Environment, Food & Rural Affairs

stories.title: Over 350 Threatened Species to Benefit from Record £90m Investment
stories.summary: DEFRA announced a £60 million three-year investment plus £30 million for species recovery on the national forest estate, benefiting over 350 threatened species across England.

stories.title: International Climate Finance Nature Spend Disclosure Published
stories.summary: DEFRA and Minister Emma Hardy MP published an Environmental Information Request (EIR) disclosure on International Climate Finance nature spending, providing transparency on the UK's climate commitments.

stories.title: Fire in Military Training Zone Spreads to Farmland
stories.summary: The Ministry of Defence confirmed a fire broke out on military training land and spread to adjacent farmland. The MoD stated fire services could not attend the original fire on military land, which initially 'burned itself out'.

stories.title: Defence Medical Research Strategy 2026–31 Published
stories.summary: The MoD published its Defence Medical Research Strategy 2026–31, setting out the demand signal for UK military medical research conducted within Defence Medical Services over the next five years.

stories.title: Post Office Capture Financial Redress: 262 Complete Applications as of 26 June 2026
stories.summary: DBT has received 262 complete applications under the Post Office Capture financial redress scheme as of 26 June 2026, reflecting the ongoing work to compensate victims of the Post Office Horizon scandal.

stories.title: DSIT and DBT Sign MoU on Digital Markets Unit Governance
stories.summary: DSIT and DBT have formalised their working relationship through a Memorandum of Understanding on the governance of the Digital Markets Unit, clarifying roles in sponsoring the Competition and Markets Authority.

agencies.name: DSIT
agencies.description: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

stories.title: DSIT Publishes Cloud Challenge Book 2026
stories.summary: DSIT launched its first Cloud Challenge Book 2026, setting out challenges across UK cloud infrastructure and inviting industry partners to innovate at national scale. The publication aims to drive ambition in UK cloud technology.

stories.title: DSIT and DBT Formalise Digital Markets Unit Governance
stories.summary: A new Memorandum of Understanding between DSIT and DBT clarifies respective departmental roles in overseeing the Digital Markets Unit under the CMA, as part of the government's wider competition and consumer policy framework.

stories.title: Burnham Urged to Ditch 'Dangerous' UK–US NHS Drug Deal
stories.summary: Health groups are calling on Andy Burnham, the expected next Prime Minister, to abandon the UK-US NHS drug deal. Analysis suggests the agreement could lead to 229,000 excess deaths by 2036 if not renegotiated.

stories.title: New Technology 'Transforming' NHS Care on 78th Anniversary
stories.summary: On the NHS's 78th anniversary, clinical directors highlight how new technology is fundamentally changing emergency and urgent care delivery, with comparisons drawn to dramatic improvements in patient outcomes.

stories.title: Public to Be Asked Who Should Pay for Social Care
stories.summary: Baroness Louise Casey, leading the government's social care review, has described the current funding system as 'impossible' and announced that the public will be consulted on who should bear the cost of social care.

stories.title: Royal Mail Confirms Changes to Delivery Service
stories.summary: Royal Mail has announced a series of changes to its delivery service. First-class post will continue to be delivered Monday to Saturday, while parcels remain unaffected by the service changes.

stories.title: Government Sets Out Future of Post Office as 'Hub of the High Street'
stories.summary: The government's response to its Post Office green paper consultation confirmed that the Post Office will trial a 'Hub of the High Street' model in 2026, positioning it as a visible economic stimulant in communities.
